comment: Common (cross-version) manual corrections for known perl C-source bugs that cause incorrect Rust binding generation. Loaded for every perl version; per-version overrides go in v$X.$Y.patches.json (kind: "remove" cancels a common entry for that version). See src/apidoc_patches.rs for the schema and doc/architecture-apidoc-patches.md for the merge model.

patches.name: RCPV_LEN
patches.reason: perl cop.h apidoc declares `RCPV *` but the macro body `(RCPVx(pv)->len-1)` returns STRLEN (a usize), not a pointer. The apidoc return type is wrong.

patches.name: RCPV_REFCOUNT
patches.reason: perl cop.h apidoc declares `RCPV *` but the macro body `(RCPVx(pv)->refcount)` returns STRLEN (the refcount field), not a pointer.

patches.name: RCPV_REFCNT_inc
patches.reason: perl cop.h apidoc declares `RCPV *` but the macro body calls rcpv_copy(pv) which returns `char *` per proto.h.

patches.name: RCPV_REFCNT_dec
patches.reason: perl cop.h apidoc declares `RCPV *` but the macro body calls rcpv_free(pv) which returns `char *` per proto.h.

patches.name: Perl_custom_op_xop
patches.reason: perl op.h macro definition `(Perl_custom_op_get_field(x, XOPe_xop_ptr).xop_ptr)` lacks `aTHX_`. The function expects 3 args (pTHX_ const OP *o, const xop_flags_enum field) but the macro only passes 2, causing Rust E0061 (function takes 3 arguments but 2 arguments were supplied). XopENTRYCUSTOM(o, xop_ptr) on line 967 is the correct alternative — it passes aTHX_ properly.
